summary_sections.title: Lesson Overview
summary_sections.content_html:
Overview

This lesson covers the fundamental measures of center used to analyze and summarize data sets. You'll learn how to calculate and apply the mean, median, and mode, and understand when each is most appropriate.

summary_sections.title: Definitions and Concepts
summary_sections.content_html:
Key Definitions

In a sugar-water solution, what is the solute?

Sugar is dissolved in water; sugar is the solute (present in smaller amount), water is the solvent.

summary_sections.title: Real-World Applications
summary_sections.content_html:
Real-World Applications

Example: Alloys like brass (copper + zinc) are examples of:

Alloys are homogeneous mixtures of metals — solid solutions.

Example: Air is an example of a:

Air is a homogeneous mixture of gases (mostly N₂ and O₂) — a gaseous solution.

Example: Which is an example of a non-electrolyte?

Glucose dissolves as intact molecules (no ions), making it a non-electrolyte. NaCl, HCl, and KOH are all electrolytes.
